user_message: Hey, can you draft the follow-up for VanArsdel Ltd?

input_artifacts.content:
From: Karen Patel <karen.patel@vanarsdelltd.com>
To: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-06 14:16 EST
Subject: Follow-up: QBR preparation Review

Thank you for the discussion last week. We'd like to move forward with QBR preparation. Can we schedule a call this week to finalize the timeline?

---
From: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
To: Karen Patel <karen.patel@vanarsdelltd.com>
Date: 2024-02-24 12:33 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: VanArsdel Ltd QBR preparation

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about QBR preparation. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: Karen Patel <karen.patel@vanarsdelltd.com>
To: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-04 09:36 EST
Subject: Re: QBR preparation Discussion - Next Steps

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with QBR preparation. What are the next steps from your side?

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF16-F trap (Ignores the trigger urgency in the original email); produces complete Thread summary, key commitments/asks identified, waiting-on-whom assessment, draft response matching
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF16-F failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF16-F failure mode (Ignores the trigger urgency in the original email)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct QBR preparation decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF16-F -- Ignores the trigger urgency in the original email
